entry.title: Linking Calendar Hierarchy and Fiscal Hierarchy Date Dimensions Using Advanced Formulas
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I need assistance with linking two models in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using advanced formulas. I am encountering an error stating "Date is using a calendar hierarchy instead of a fiscal year." Here are the details of my situation:

  1. Models:

    • Finance Model[Default Model]: Uses a calendar hierarchy with year, quarter, and month.
    • OPEX Model: Uses a fiscal hierarchy with fiscal year, quarter, and period.

Date([d/Measures] = "SUM" = LINK [OPEX], [d/Measures] = "SUM", [d/Date] = [d/Date])

I need guidance on the correct approach and syntax to link these two date dimensions.

Thanks,

Pooja.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere OData Consumption API for Analytic Model
entry.content:
Dear community,

We are trying to consume the OData Endpoint of an analytic model. Because of Data Access Controls (DAC) applied to the underlying Fact View, we would also like to consume this data with an end users mail address/username. This way, based on the performer of the API Call, different data is pushed back.

When doing this in the browser, this works perfectly fine (see pictures).

However, we would like to use this data in a fiori application. For this, we want to automate the authorization flow but it seems like Datasphere is not accepting the token fetching in an automated way. Is there a way to login using client credentials OAuth?

Is this something related to the Client setup in Datasphere (Interactive/API Access?)

Kind regards,

Cas

entry.title: Negative Stock Configuration and Handling in SAP MM
entry.content:
Negative Stock Configuration and Handling in SAP MM

Negative Stock:

Negative stocks are allowed when the goods issue needs to be done urgently. Just due to organizational reasons Goods Issues are entered before than the corresponding Goods receipts.

  • Once the goods receipts have been posted, the book inventory balance must again correspond to the physical stock, that is, the book inventory balance may no longer be negative.
  • Negative stocks are always a sign that physical movements must be entered in the system at a later stage.

In SAP we can handle Negative Stock Handling by using 2 Scenario:-

  • Create PO with Activating negative stock in plant in material master Goods issue with movement type 201 and then GR by Movement type 101.
  • Create PO with Activating negative stock in plant in material master Goods issue with movement type 201 and then Transfer posting from one storage location to Another with Movement type 311.

Scenario: 1

 

Scenario:2

Important Points to be Remember:

  • You can also manage negative stock balances for materials with moving average price control and for special stocks, such as vendor consignment, material provided to vendor, sales order stock, and project stock.
  • Negative stock balances are required if, for organizational reasons, goods issues are entered before the corresponding goods receipts, and the material is already physically located in the warehouse.
  • Once you have posted the goods receipts, the book inventory balance should correspond with the physical stock again – in other words, it should no longer be negative. Negative stocks always indicate that physical movements have to be entered in the system at a later stage.
  • If you work with negative stock balances, you have to ensure that you do not have negative stocks on the balance sheet key date or when you carry out a physical inventory.

Prerequisites for Negative Stock Handling:

In order to use negative stocks, you have to allow negative stock in customizing.

Only those materials for which negative stocks is activated in the material master, will be

allowed to have negative stocks.

  • So that negative stocks are allowed, the 'Negative stocks' indicator should be marked into the material master data via MM01/MM02 transactions on General Plant Data 2 view at ‘General Plant Parameters’ screen.
  • Customizing: transaction OMJ1 - Allowing Negative Stocks.

First level:

Valuation area: Choose for which valuation area you want to allow negative stocks and activate the flag a. Select the line of that valuation area and go to the plant level.

If we cannot maintain Plant in the Plant Parameters, the system does not show any Plant. So first check in the Plant Parameters while doing this step.

Plant Parameter:

Configuration: SPRO –> IMG –> Material Management –> Inventory Management and Physical Inventory –> Plant Parameters

We can maintain the Plant by clicking on the New Entries or by copying from the standard one. After maintaining the plant click on the Save button.

After Maintaining the Plant in the Plant Parameters, the system triggers the Plant.

Here I can activate the Negative Stock allow for RM01 Storage Location.

Creation of Material in MM01:

In the General Plant Data/Storage 2 view we can activate the negative stock allow for the material for the Plant.

Check the Stock Overview for the Material in MMBE before doing Goods Issue.

Right now, we don’t have any stock.

Goods Issue –> Other in MIGO

After Maintaining the required data click on the Post or Save button.

Material document 400000001 posted.

The Accounting Entries are:

  • As we have no stock of this material in our system & we do goods issue without stock now the stock of material showing in Negative 50.
  • When we do Goods Receipt for this material system will automatically adjust the negative stock.

Now we can create the Purchase order in ME21N.

PO - Material created under the number 2000055.

We have 200 Quantity of On-Order Stock after the Creation of PO. When we do Goods Receipt w.r.t Purchase Order the 150 Quantity of stock will update in the Unrestricted use.

Goods Receipt –> Purchase Order

Material document 1 posted.

The Accounting Entries are:

Now check the stock again in MMBE.

  • Now the system will be showing positive stock of 150 Quantity.
  • As we are doing Goods Receipt of 200 Quantity.
  • System will adjust the negative stock after the Goods Receipt.

Scenario:2

  • In this Scenario, we have two storage locations under the same Plant and have the stock in both Storage locations, but we need to consume more quantity than the stock available then we can activate the Negative Stock for the Storage location and in the Material Master.
  • In the Next Step we can do Goods Issue w.r.t Others with Movement Type 201. Later we can transfer the stock from one Storage Location to Another Storage Location with Movement Type 311.

Process flow:

  • Activate the Negative Handling in another Storage location and Activate the Negative stock tab in Material Master.

Here we have the stock in both Storage locations.

In the MMBE we have 50 Qty of stock in FG01 storage location but I need more 50 Qty to move for production or to deliver in that case we used Negative Stock Handling Scenario.

If you do Goods Issue w.r.t Others without activating the Negative Handling tab in the Material Master, the system throws an error like this:

Activate the Negative Stocks for Storage location in OMJ1 Transaction.

Next Activate the Negative stock in Material Master record and click on the Save.

Now do Goods Issue w.r.t Others

Material document 400000002 posted.

The Accounting Entries are:

If you check the stock in MMBE the stock is updated with the Negative Quantity.

 

                  

In the very next step, we can transfer the stock from one storage location to another Storage location with the Movement Type 311.

Material document 400000003 posted.

No Documents Entries were generated.

Now check the stock in MMBE the 30 Qty of stock is updated in the FG01 Storage location and RM01 storage stock is reduced to 70 Qty.

entry.title: LTMC – Legacy Transfer Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
LTMC – Legacy Transfer Migration Cockpit

LTMC:

LTMC is a tool used to load data into SAP S/4HANA system. There you can define projects to do the uploading with different options. SAP has created several predefined data migration objects that can be included into the projects.

For data migration objects not yet defined or custom data migration objects, you can use SAP S/4HANA Migration Object Modeler (T.code LTMOM), there you can define (create or modify) objects to use in SAP S/4HANA Migration cockpit.

Data Migration:

Data migration is a process of transforming data from heterogeneous systems to heterogeneous targets with lots of information.

The Data Migration process includes:

  • Extraction of data from the source system.
  • Transformation of the data to SAP format.
  • Loading of data into the corresponding SAP module.

Introduction:

In a new implementation of SAP S/4HANA system, data is migrated from the SAP system/Non-SAP system to SAP S/4HANA system.

SAP S/4HANA provides comprehensive migration solutions with no programming requirement by the customer, automated mapping of data between source and target system, predefined templates, easily integrated custom objects, reduce test effort and minimize downtime.

Following are different options available for data migration in SAP S/4HANA on-premises:

  • SAP Data Services (SAP DS)
  • SAP Rapid Data Migration Content
  • SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit with Migration object modeler

SAP Data Services:
SAP DS is an ETL tool from SAP, which was a huge success for Data warehouse and data migration which follows the methodology as E-T-L (Extraction, Transformation, and Load). It also provides data of quality management and text data processing. The ETL capabilities of the tool can be used free of charge, but the data quality features require a Data Services license.

SAP Rapid Data Migration:
The rapid data migration to SAP S/4HANA solution enables you to realize the full benefits of our next-generation business suite even faster. It accelerates the process of cleansing, validating, transforming, and migrating your data from any system to SAP S/4HANA.

SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit (MC):

SAP S/4HANA Migration cockpit is an automated migration tool provided with pre-configured content and mapping for each migration object. For all major functional areas like a business partner, product, customer, bank, profit center, cost center, plant, GL, etc.

Migration objects are predefined, and their sequence and dependency are also defined. Each migration object contains its own predefined template in which source data is uploaded. Automatic mapping is done in source and target structures using the template. Migration programs are automatically generated for validating data, simulating data and loading into the target system

Key Features of Migration Cockpit:

  • This tool is embedded and delivered with SAP S/4HANA system.
  • No programming is required by the customer.
  • This tool is used to migrate data from SAP or Non-SAP system to SAP S/4HANA.
  • This tool has a predefined migration object which contains a mapping for all master/transactional data. It reduces migration cost and time.
  • Migration activities are predefined and easy to use.

In S/4 HANA LTMC is divided in two Parts:

  • LTMC – Legacy Transfer Migration Cockpit
  • LTMOM – Legacy Transfer Migration Object Modeler

LTMOM:

There are a few customization fields that must be added, where LTMC can’t help then LTMOM has come to the rescue of the situation here.

LTMOM will allow us to create additional fields and customization fields in the source structure and mapped to the target structure.

S/4 HANA Migration Cockpit Process Flow

Let’s Create the business Partner by using the LTMC in S4 HANA:

Enter the Transaction Code as LTMC in the Command Field.

LTMC is open on the web page not on the SAP GUI.

Click on the create button to create the new Project.

Enter the Project Name and select the transfer options from where to upload the data.

Mass Transfer ID: It is generated automatically based on existing projects count, if you want your own customized ID, please click on “Generate New Mass Transfer ID”.

Click on the create button which is shown below to create the Project.

The System shows that the Project is created but not started

Select the line and click on the open tab.

Enter as Supplier in the search tab, the system shows the standard object. Double click on the object

The system shows Pop-up Message.

 Click on ok button I.e., The standard data is copied to our customized Project.

Now see the status changes from Not Started to In process.

Now Download the Template, click on the Download Template Tab which is present in the source files as shown below.

Click on save

After Downloading the Template, click on open the Template.

Ensure that the download Template is relevant or not.

In this step, we can see what fields are mandatory and optional

The tabs with * mark mean the fields are mandatory fields that must need to be filled.

We can take 00001 as a Reference because the number range interval length is “5”

By Maintaining all the required data in the file Upload the File.

Click on Upload File tab

Click on the Browse tab to Assign the file and Enter the Description and click on Upload.

After Uploading the file, select the line item and click on the start tab.

If you want to Deactivate the process, click on the Deactivate tab or else click on the start Transfer.

After clicking on the Start Transfer Tab the system sends the Pop-up message that the data from all files that have status “Not Started” or “In Process” will now be transferred.

Click on the OK tab.

After clicking on OK tab, the system fletched all the data that has maintained in file.

Click on the close tab after progress is completed.

If the data contains any Errors, the system shows the errors message that the data is missed at some location. If all the data is maintained correct, click on the Next tab which is shown below.

In this step the system asks to confirm the Mapping Values. It means to check the data that we maintained in the file and whether the data which is showing is similar or not.

Check the Each line by clicking on the Each line item.

After checking all the data click on the next tab which is shown as above.

The Data simulation progress takes place. After completion of Data simulation Progress click on the close tab.

Click on the next tab which is shown below.

The Data will be Imported. After Completion of Data Import in Progress.

Click on close which is shown below.

The system will update you, All files have been Processed clock on the finish tab which is shown below.

After clicking on the tab, the system status changes from in progress to Finished.

The Vendors Created successfully.

If you want to make any fields optional or to hide. You can Customize that fields by using LTMOM

Enter the Transaction code as LTMOM in the command field. Click on Enter.

Select the Source structure and change the screen from Display mode to change Mode.

In the Header select the Migration Object and click on the Display – Change Mode.

Migration Object –> Display <–> Change

In the Display option tab, there is a field named Change view.

Click on the Change View field.

If you want to make any changes in the fields (I.e., Not visible/Visible/Required) make the changes and click on Save button.

This is the Overall Process of LTMC (Legacy Transfer Migration Cockpit)

entry.title: The Epic Go-Live Blunder: When Master Data Went on a QAS Vacation
entry.content:
During the project phase, calamity struck when I logged into the incorrect system QAS rather than PRD. I unintentionally migrated all of the master data to QAS. Thinking everything was alright, I checked PRD and discovered it was empty, which made me fear.

After further investigation, I understood my error: I placed all the crucial data in the incorrect location. It felt like shipping a package to the wrong address when it was too late.

Feeling ashamed, I quickly attempted to resolve the issue, understanding that technological mistakes happen.

With determination, I corrected my error, likening it to a digital game of hot potato.

Finally, as I hit "submit," I laughed at how a minor login error caused so much confusion. Technology may surprise and educate us.

entry.title: How to Find Best Practice Content for SAP Environment, Health and Safety for Public Cloud
entry.content:
If you want to implement a SAP Environment, Health and Safety solution on S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, or you're just curios about it, a good start is to check out the Best Practice content. One of the most valuable things you find there are test scripts explaining you how to work with the solutions on concrete examples.

 

  1. Open the Signavio Process Navigator in SAP for Me: https://me.sap.com/processnavigator 
  2. Choose Solution Scenarios.

     

  3. Choose SAP Best Practices for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in the product area Enterprise Resource Planning.

     

  4. Expand Manufacturing and Choose Environment, Health and Safety.



  5. Choose the EHS solution that you're interested in, for example Emissions Management.
  6. Choose Accelerators.



  7. Download the test script in your preferred language.

     

  8. Done. 

If you have a S/4HANA Cloud starter tenant, you can try out the test scripts in this system.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
It seems you have added some additional partitioning in your SQL statement, so it is not getting row numbers for the whole table but for smaller partitions.

Could you restore it to

INNER JOIN 
  (SELECT 
     ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS "row_num", *
   FROM {source_table}
  ) AS "B"

and then re-run the cell?

entry.title: Re: EBR (Log Buyoffs) in Process Industry in SAP Digital...
entry.content:
Hi,

Log Buyoff plugin is currently not supported in Order POD, see Plugins by POD Type.
EBR capabilities are planned in DM roadmap, so keep an eye on that for the future.
Running process orders in parallel in both Work Center and Order PODs is not supported, as each of theses PODs require very specific setup and works in different ways, see note #3469745 for more details.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP
entry.content:
@anil_lahori2 

Now that Managed BO with Unmanaged Save is available, I think there are hardly any use cases for a pure unmanaged scenario. You would use Managed BO with Unmanaged Save when you want to implement your own logic (e.g., using existing code or calling standard RAP BOs) for saving data to the database (or an external system), rather than having the RAP framework handle the saving.

entry.title: Re: Convert plan order to process order from MD04
entry.content:
Hi,

In OPPQ or OPPR, it seems that you have not defined the “Process order” type for the conversion. Retest by assigning the relevant order type. For OPPR also check whether you have assigned the correct MRP group to the material.

entry.title: Re: Convert plan order to process order from MD04
entry.content:
Hi,

if you convert a planned order into a process order the the Prod. Sched. Profile needs to be set to a PI related one.
I guess this is not set right now hence you get the message as PP01 is by standard used.
Set it to PI01 and run it again.

BTW: A order conversion of materials the are PPDS relevant should be performed from PPDS and not from S4.
PPDS is the scheduling leading system and the dates from that system needs to be taken over in the S4core system. In order to keep the ORDID a conversion from PPDS needs to be done. If you convert the order in S4 then a new order is used hence a not ORDID and that "can" lead to a rescheduling.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne July '24
entry.content:
Melbourne! we are back again for the 3rd time this year. Now in the thick of winter, the perfect time to get out and hang with your SAP buddies.

This time we are going to mix it up a bit and make it a Wednesday in case Thursdays don't work for your regular weekly commitments. The other bonus is that it is $28 Parma & Schooner night! but there are other good deals if you grab an early dinner too.

So far this year (in March & May) we have had a great mix of people at the Melbourne Stammtische, and alway great conversation. I have really enjoyed the relaxed and friendly interactions. It's been brilliant catching up with familiar faces, those I haven't seen for a long time and new ones.

We are now on par with meeting about every two months, with the idea to keep it consistent and provide ample opportunity for people to be able to make it, or just catch up more often. We are not too worried about getting big numbers, but more focused on good interactions. And on that front it has been succeeding, so I'm really looking forward to this next gathering. I hope to see you there, and be sure to spread the word around.

Wednesday 24th July 2024 at 5:30pm at The Bank on Collins 

 

 Ground floor/394 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000.

 

When you arrive, if you can't find us you can ask for a booking for the "SAP Regulars Table" 

Ben.

entry.title: Use TypeScript with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
entry.content:
Use TypeScript with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

When I started covering the SAP Cloud Application Model topic as a Developer Advocate, I had to start working with a programming language I had tried to avoid over the past years: JavaScript.
All my past development experience was around type-safe programming languages like Swift or GoLang. Type-safe means that the compiler does type-checking on your code. The compiler will validate types while compiling and throw an error if a type is assigned or used invalidly, like dividing a number by an array of Strings. Jumping into JavaScript made my head hurt quite a bit because it allowed me, at least most of the time, to do whatever I felt like doing. Only during runtime or within my unit tests did my code throw errors. Unfortunately, these errors also didn't help me understand what was happening.

const obj = { width: 10, height: 15 };


const area = obj.width * obj.heigth;
// This will result in NaN.

You can see where JavaScript comes from and what it was initially intended for - writing small, self-contained code scripts to complete smaller, concise tasks like handling a button press. It was created to make websites dynamic and enhance the user experience by providing advanced, interactive features. Over time, more and more functionality was added to the language because developers started to write more extensive code to achieve complicated tasks within the browser. JavaScript also evolved into a language that allowed for code to run on the server side with the Node.js flavor. Having server-side software run with a language that provides no compile-time type-safety felt like it would make code harder to maintain, understand, and develop.

I appreciate where JavaScript comes from and see its benefits, like its speed, its ability to be used in many use cases and environments, and its power to give the developer a lot of freedom. However, I am more comfortable having a safety net below me, so I jumped onto a JavaScript flavor, adding a static type-checker during design time—TypeScript.

What is TypeScript?

TypeScript provides a static type-checker around the JavaScript core. It helps make code more readable by allowing the definition of types in code and adding the possibility to define object types through interfacing and compile-time type checking. The beauty is that TypeScript does not alternate the underlying JavaScript code. Drop your working JavaScript code one-to-one into a TypeScript file, and your code will run and compile. TypeScript is not altering the JavaScript core; it is adding type safety. When you compile and build your code, TypeScript will remove your type declarations so that the compiled binary only includes pure JavaScript. That means you get all the creative possibilities and power JavaScript provides, but it allows you to add a safety net when doing your acrobatic endeavors.

let number = 5
let string = "hello"

number = string
// The compiler will warn you that "string is not assignable to type the number."

How do I get started?

Using TypeScript within your CAP projects is easy, and I will show you how!

  1. Install the TypeScript npm package for NodeJS.
npm i -g typescript ts-node
  1. Create a tsconfig.json file. Within that file, you can define JavaScript runtime environment dependencies and compiler options for TypeScript. e.g.:
{
    "extends": "@tsconfig/node12/tsconfig.json",
    "compilerOptions": {
      "preserveConstEnums": true
    },
    "include": ["src/**/*"],
    "exclude": ["**/*.spec.ts"]
  }

  1. Write your code in .ts files.

  2. Use the cds-ts command instead of cds to invoke TypeScript instead of plain JavaScript.

    The cds-ts command is being used to avoid precompiling TypeScript files to JavaScript on each execution; this will speed up the development process. For production, use cds to build the final binary!

CAP & TypeScript by example

If we use the Hello World example from the official CAP documentation, you would build a TypeScript CAP application like this:

  • Service Definition (world.cds):
service say @(path: '/say') {
  function hello (to:String) returns String;
}
  • TypeScript implementation (world.ts):
import { Request } from "@sap/cds"

module.exports = class say {
    hello(req: Request) {
        return `Hello ${req.data.to} from a TypeScript file!`
    }
}

The complete example can be found in the SAP-samples repository.

Conclusion

JavaScript is a powerful language widely used in web development, server-side programming, and many more places! It is an industry-standard, and millions of developers love it. However, it feels chaotic and gives way too much freedom to developers, so I decided to switch to TypeScript. TypeScript will provide the power of JavaScript but it'll help me better understand the code written and ensure that I don't do something silly. It is an excellent addition to programming with Node.js and CAP daily. 

I hope you enjoyed this little blog post about TypeScript, and I hope you will give it a try!

With that, keep Coding!

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2408
entry.content:
Overview

Providing a significant number of new features is an important goal of release upgrades to deliver innovations to our customers and partners. There are four major release upgrades per year following a predefined schedule as described in this blog post.

An upgrade is intended as a “no event” for customers and partners. For example, only compatible changes of released objects (APIs) will be delivered. We recommend performing a test of the custom-built productive apps in a test environment for the new release. The complexity of the technical dependencies between the custom-built apps and the underlying environment cannot be tested isolated, only including the ABAP environment on SAP-side. Therefore, we offer you to pre-upgrade selected systems 15 days in advance to test the applications prior to the planned standard-upgrade.

 

Purpose

This option enables a customer or partner to test the applications they have already developed on top of the new release a couple of days before the standard-upgrade. With SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk"), we follow the approach of keeping released objects stable and provide all enhancements in a compatible fashion. Early testing is an additional measure to ensure proper software quality and might be used to detect unforeseen regressions. However, this option is not intended to test new features or to consume new features earlier.

 

Approach

The standard-upgrade of all systems to release 2408 is planned for August 17/18.

A customer or partner can nominate one or more already existing systems for the pre-upgrade. A nominated system will be upgraded on August 2 within a planned downtime from 8:00 to 16:00 CET.

We recommend nominating a non-development system, such as a test system. The nomination of a development system is technically also possible, but fixes or other changes must not be imported into another system with a lower release. The nomination of a productive system doesn’t make sense because the purpose of the pre-upgrade is the re-test of the existing application before the productive system gets the new release.

According to the approach explained above, this offer is only relevant for customers or partners having at least two systems.

There might be situations where a correction of custom objects needs to be deployed. Such corrections are possible if the development / correction system is on the older release and is imported into the pre-upgraded system with the higher release.

Except potential fixes, the pre-upgrade is based on the same version as the standard-upgrade.

 

Costs

This offering is optional and free of any additional charges.

 

Planned New Features

The planned new features will be published on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment on July 19. This list gives you an overview of potential areas for re-testing. The purpose of the pre-upgrade is not to consume these features before the standard-upgrade, because the documentation on help.sap.com will be delivered later before the standard-upgrade. It would also not be possible to transport code of such an adoption, because subsequent systems are not yet on the new release during the pre-upgrade phase.

Independent of the upgrades of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we recommend developing automated tests for the custom-built apps to detect regressions early on and without manual effort for re-testing. Such automated tests are perfectly suited to be run in the pre-upgrade test phase.

 

Time Schedule

The following figure summarizes the general approach:

 

Milestones for release 2408:

  • July 5: Nomination of system(s) is possible in the Landscape Portal
  • July 19: Announcement of the planned new features
  • July 29: Pre-upgrade systems are known
  • August 2: Pre-upgrade
  • August 14: Potential decision to postpone the upgrade
  • August 17/18: Standard-upgrade

 

System Nomination

To nominate a system, please register the system(s) by July 29 (EOB) latest by using the Landscape Portal. You can find further information about Accessing the Landscape Portal and about Register Systems for Pre-Upgrade. Please do not create customer tickets for system nomination like in previous pre-upgrades.

With such a nomination, the customer agrees to an extraordinary planned maintenance on August 2 (see above).

 

Report an Issue

Issues should be reported as part of the regression test phase by August 9 (EOB) to allow for a sufficient time frame for issue analysis and potential corrections on SAP-side.

Please create a customer ticket by using component BC-CP-ABA.

If a detected issue is likely to endanger the productive use after the standard-upgrade, you should use ticket priority very high despite the fact that the pre-upgrade system is not a productive system.

 

Exceptional Process in Case of Unresolved Priority Very High Issues

In case of the aligned decision on Wednesday, August 14 (based on unresolved priority very high tickets) to postpone the upgrade, a dedicated mitigation process needs to be started together with the respective customer or partner, with the following boundary conditions:

  • Resolving the open priority very high issues remains the highest priority for both, SAP and the customer or partner, as it is also the prerequisite for the upgrade.
  • According to the estimated issue resolution, catching up the upgrade needs to be planned.
  • The upgrade leads to a business downtime of 6 – 8 hours.
  • The upgrade must be scheduled within 7 calendar days the latest after fixing all remaining open priority very high issues.
  • We will not provide any fixes for the old release after the original date of the standard-upgrade to the new release.

In case business-critical issues are detected after the upgrade:

  • Issues need to be reported as priority very high issues and will be processed accordingly.
  • Going back to the release before upgrade is not an option. Therefore, resolving the issues in the new release with the appropriate priority is the required approach.

 

Summary

We recommend performing regression tests in pre-upgraded test systems to avoid day-1 impacts of productive custom apps after the standard-upgrade.

entry.title: SAP script error checking
entry.content:
Hi,

When a command is send to SAP like "SAPsession.FindById("wnd[0]").resizeWorkingPane(170, 25, False)" and an error occurs I'd like to log the error and let the script continue after user input (if needed) to fix the error. Next I want to use the Log to examine the errors and fix them.

I was thinking to do this via an additional sub that handles the sap command and catches the error like this:

****************************************************************

Private sub resize() 'SAPCommand to be executed

SAPCommand(SAPsession.FindById("wnd[0]").resizeWorkingPane(170, 25, False))
SAPCommand(SAPsession.FindById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nsu3")
SAPCommand(SAPsession.FindById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(0))

End Sub

 

Private sub SAPCommand(input)         '//SAPCommand to be executed

try
    Run SAPCommand
Catch ex as Exception
    Log("Error for: " & input & vbCRLF & ex.tostring)
    msgbox("Fix the error for command: " & input)
end try

end sub

****************************************************************

I know the above code does not workout at all but it's just for illustration of what I'd like to accomplish.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Personal migration journey BTP: from Workflow Management to Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello @smarchesini ,

If you use bus. rules, just for data, which you can define on dev (where edit version is), then it is ok. But in WFM I used to use it for rule, where I defined system level where the process is (dev, test or prod). So on test, I need to put there string "test". I use it eg. for adding some additional watermark texts into mail notifications - like "This is TEST appravl only!" - I`m not able to find other way how to decide from a process, on which tenant (subaccount) it is run. Of course it can be some external service, like feature flags etc..., But thats not good - to add other dependency.

But recently I found in a roadmap, that decision tables should be modifiable.

Neverthless, because of lack of other options, I made a workaround - I created secodn process with one decision. I export/import the editable version of that process into every tenant (dev, test, prod), so I can change it directly on each system. And after deployment, you can call that decision by api. This serves its purpose, but not as elegant, as it should/would be when in process itself.

entry.title: En empleados con tramos no se calcula bien la base de cotización en el tramo que supera el tope máx.
entry.content:
Hola, buen día,


Por la naturaleza de la actividad de la empresa, solemos tener muchos empleados cuya nómina mensual está dividida por tramos y hemos detectado que en empleados topados, al llegar al tramo donde se supera la base de cotización de este año en España, SAP no ajusta bien la base de cotización de modo que, agregando las bases de cotización calculadas en tramos anteriores a la base de este último tramo donde se supera la base máxima de cotización se llegue a dicha base máxima. Lo que se calcula en ese último tramo es inferior a lo que debería salir para que la suma de todas las bases calculadas del mes alcancen el total de la base máxima de cotización.
 
Un cordial saludo. Muchas gracias.
Alberto Carpintero

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP (Bangalore, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP-based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on-premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment.

Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday 10th July 2024 in Bengaluru, India from 9:00 - 16:00 IST. 

Location: Deloitte India, Floor 18, Prestige Towers, Bangalore Branch & ATM, 99/100, Residency Rd, Shanthala Nagar, Ashok Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560025

The event is kindly being hosted by Deloitte India, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page.

The instructors are Developer Advocates Sheena M K , Ajay Soreng and Shilpa Shankar. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: ODataModel not getting called when adding authorization header
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a weird problem that suddently appeared. We have an fiori application where we call a OData secured by a JWT Bearer token. We added the token when creating the odata model.

 

                    const oDataModel = new ODataModel({
                        "serviceUrl": "/odata/v2",
                        "headers": {
                            "Authorization": "Bearer " + accessToken.AccessKey
                        }
                    });

 

This has worked for us up until last Monday, when it suddenly stopped working from within Business Application Studio.

In WebDeveloperTools I can see that the response is a 401, that takes much shorter time than expected from the API

I cannot see the call in the Terminal

 

info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$metadata?sap-language=EN
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService2>/$metadata?sap-language=EN
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch

 

 

If I however comment out the Authorization header in my call, or even replace the header name with anything else it behaves as expected. I get a popup asking for Basic Auth, and I can see the call in the terminal.

 

info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$metadata?sap-language=EN
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$metadata?sap-language=EN
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch
info backend-proxy-middleware /odata/v2/$metadata         <-------------
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch

 

 

I don't understand what is happening, but to me it seems like either Chrome or backend-proxy-middleware suddenly filters out calls with an Authorization header.  It seems like it's working on our version deployed on a Gateway Hub, so I tend to think it's the backend-proxy-middleware that does something.

Regards
Mattias

entry.title: No need to reinvent the wheel to improve citizen service.
entry.content:
I like the term the citizen consumer.  Each one of us is a citizen and also a consumer.  We deal with governments at multiple levels (local, state, federal) and we deal with companies as consumers / customers.  Commercial companies are in fierce competition for our money, so they need to provide excellent services and experiences to win us as customers.  We, as citizens, want the same service that we get as consumers when we deal with our government.

Governments know this and are acting. The US Federal Government has passed an executive order to transform customer experience and service delivery while the German government has passed the Online Access Act  (Onlinezugangsgesetz, OZG).

Governments are very different from commercial companies in many ways and have many unique processes and requirements. Despite this, governments can still use commercial software for many processes, often with few  modifications or customizations.

A good example is the SAP Endorsed App, LIVE CONTRACT Public by SYNCPILOT. 

LIVE CONTRACT Public by SYNCPILOT is a platform for governments to provide online digital services to their citizens. It will enable ad-hoc meetings as well as provide online appointment scheduling where citizens are assigned to an available agent using skill matching to best fulfill the citizen needs. 

During the online meeting , the government worker and citizen can interact barrier-free and share information through presentations, co-browsing, screensharing, whiteboards, videos, voice, etc.  They can even complete and legally sign documents / forms electronically and securely together in real time - without changing media.  The use of voice and video is done entirely in LIVE CONTRACT without the need for any additional 3rd party software / systems.​ LIVE CONTRACT will enable governments to digitalize and improve service with minimal effort as it was designed to seamlessly integrate into existing back-office systems and processes. It can be deployed rapidly without the need to replace existing back-office systems.

Built on top of the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) LIVE CONTRACT Public by SYNCPILOT is a SaaS offering that supports strong data privacy policies and is GDPR compliant. SYNCPILOT is the winner of the SAP Global SAP Customer Experience Partner Excellence Award 2022 for SAP Business Technology Platform Customer Success.

The City of Heidelberg is already using LIVE CONTRACT as “Virtuelles Bürgeramt” to improve the service for its citizens. –You can find a 2-minute video here showcasing its use. It is in German language but you can switch on closed captions and auto translate to any language.

More details can be found on the SAP Store listing and on the SYNCPILOT site.

Please send me a message here or reply with comment below for more information and to discuss further.

entry.title: Edit Component Text in the UI Canvas 📝
entry.content:
Just released! You can edit a component's text attributes from within the UI canvas, without going to the Properties pane.

This seems to be true of any component with a text type property, like titles, text, and cards.

The text seems to be editable in the canvas if the binding type is static text or a simple text formula without any functions. Otherwise, you have to manually change the binding type to static text.

entry.title: Problem: Multiple Freight Settlement creation in SAP TM
entry.content:
For 1 freight order the system creates automatically different freight settlements to different dates automatically and that is not correct.

It should be that for 1 Freight order the system creates only 1 freight settlement document.

The steps are following in the system:

When departed it makes automatic goods issue of the delivery note.

Furthermore 1 Freight settlement is automatically created.

When the GI is registered then 1 correct freight settlement is automatically created (via JOBPLANER)

This example was correct.

But suddenly to a later point for example 03.01.2024 or 28.12.2023 a further freight settlement is automatically created. And that is not correct.

The yellow marked freight settlement documents were created automatically without any change in the system.

Here are some Screenshots:

 

I checked already the customizing and the master data I did not find an issue in these areas.

Is there maybe an SAP Note? Or is it there anything in Coding ABAP?

How is it possible to solve this problem?

Thanks a lot.

I recommend to access our Exploring the SAP Master Data Governance Deployments learning journey

Overview
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to evaluate common business scenarios and suggest the SAP Master Data Governance deployment option that should be applied for each scenario.

Learning objectives
In this learning journey, you will explore the business relevance of master data management and the different deployment options available for SAP Master Data Governance.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Margit

entry.title: Re: Linking Calendar Hierarchy and Fiscal Hierarchy Date...
entry.content:
@pooja_elangovan01 

The LINK function might not work if the date dimension in both model are of different hierarchy types. If its a user defined date dimension then in Fiscal date dimension, you can try maintaining an attribute with fiscal to calendar mapping. So maintain the calendar months against the calendar periods. Then try setting up the LINK as below

 

Date([d/Measures] = "SUM" = LINK [OPEX], [d/Measures] = "SUM", [d/Date] = [d/Date].[p/calendarperiod])

calendarperiod is the attribute. 

Hopefully this may work.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAC story pie chart does not show data labels/ legend...
entry.content:
@Chr_Frier 

Try following the below note:

3420049 - Pie Chart is not showing legend for single dimension in SAP Analytics cloud

Excerpts from Note:

Reproducing the Issue

  1. Log in to SAC.
  2. Open the story with a Pie chart.

Notice that the legend is not visible in the pie chart.

Cause

Legend is not enabled for the single dimension.

Resolution

Follow the below steps to see the legend :

  1. In the Pie chart click on 3 dots.
  2. Click on Show and hide.
  3. Enable the legend option.
  4. Save the story.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 21 - Maintenance Logistics with SAP EWM
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog post for the SAP EWM Podcast Episode 21 – Maintenance Logistics with SAP EWM.

Maintenance is a topic that is basically closely linked to the warehouse topic: be it the forklifts, the components of the conveyor systems or other equipment that is installed in the warehouse – everything has to be maintained somehow at some point in time. And of course, there are also specific warehouses, which store the components for the maintenance of machines in production or any other type of asset – and for these warehouses a warehouse management solution is mandatory.

For this use case, it is important that Warehouse processes integrate directly with Maintenance processes, to ensure stock transparency or to enable direct picking of spare parts for maintenance orders.

A modern and intuitive warehouse management solution like SAP EWM helps those warehouses, to ensure error-free work, to automate processes and to gain 100% transparency and traceability.

SAP Plant Maintenance and SAP Enterprise Asset Management

If we talk about maintenance logistics with SAP EWM  another SAP module plays an important role. SAP Plant Maintenance (SAP PM) which is part of the umbrella solution SAP Enterprise Asset Management (SAP EAM).  

SAP Enterprise Asset Management helps customers to achieve maximum asset availability with the longest possible life of the asset. This means reducing the downtime of assets and resources while avoiding costly spare parts procurement. It provides tools for managing and structuring technical objects such as assets, assemblies, equipment and so on.

And SAP EAM also supports customers in maintenance and repair. These can be planned or recurring inspections or it could be more sophisticated strategies like predictive maintenance.

In addition, SAP EAM also takes care of the execution which is usually done with the so-called maintenance order. This is a request to perform a maintenance task on a maintenance object on a specific date. In addition, the maintenance order is a tool for the documentation of maintenance tasks. The order is used to

  • plan specific maintenance
  • monitor the implementation of maintenance and
  • record and account for the costs incurred as a result of maintenance

The data of the order is included in the maintenance history and is therefore of the utmost importance for evaluations and future planning of such measures.

Integration points between SAP EWM and SAP EAM

With SAP EWM, companies can optimize warehouse logistics for service parts and maintenance components. 

Improved Stock Management
It is important especially for unplanned and urgent maintenance to have 100% stock transparency and quick access to the spare parts. In addition, maintenance often also stores expensive spare parts, which are sometimes also managed by serial numbers.

With SAP EWM, customers can manage stocks in a storage bin-specific, batch-specific, serial-number-specific, and also load carrier-specific. In particular, the batches and serial numbers are then also recorded exactly in the context of traceability during repair or replacement.

100% Stock Transparency with SAP EWM

Improved Picking and Staging
As mentioned above, maintenance orders are created in SAP Enterprise Asset Management for planned or unplanned maintenance. These orders also contain the components and spare parts required for the process, which are stored in an EWM-managed warehouse. These components can be entered manually in the order or they can be determined via a task list.

The maintenance order automatically triggers a reservation for its components, for which a staging must take place. The result of the staging, if it is an EWM-managed storage location, is then an outbound delivery that is transferred to EWM.

Special document types can then be found in EWM for these outbound deliveries - technically, this is solved via a special initiator process code for maintenance, which is transferred from the ERP to EWM via the standard interface. 

This makes it easy to configure special processes for picking and staging in the warehouse for maintenance outbound deliveries. And this is important because when we talk about the picking and staging of maintenance components, various processes are conceivable.

  • Pick-up by a service technician directly in the warehouse
  • Staging of the components to a specific asset or machine to be maintained or to be moved to a work center in a specific building
  • Shipping of the components to an external address 

Staging of Maintenance Components with SAP EWM

The object "Recipient Location"
To better organize these processes, a new object – the recipient location – has been defined in the integration between EAM and EWM. It is defined at plant level and can be assigned to each maintenance component and it is comparable to the production supply area from the production supply process.

SAP EWM uses the recipient location to determine a specific staging area where, for example, the service technician can pick up the components. Therefore the following steps are required: 

  1. Define possible recipient locations as master data in the system
  2. Assign recipient locations to the maintenance order items
  3. Define a staging area determination in SAP EWM based on the recipient location for the outbound delivery items

Recipient Location

The actual staging and therefore the creation of the outbound delivery can take place, for example, using transaction MB26 or MIGO  - automatically in background or manual.

It is not necessary to specify batches or serial numbers. The selection can be made by logistics within the SAP EWM stock removal strategy or during picking. In addition, the transaction checks whether outbound deliveries have already been created and prevents a multiple staging for the same maintenance order.

In SAP EWM features like wave management, picking order  creation, packing, and staging can then be used without restrictions to optimize the warehouse processes.

With the goods issue posting, the consumption posting of the components to the maintenance order takes place.

SAP PM-EWM Integration - Document Flow for Staging

 

Demo Video of SAP EWM - SAP PM Integration
The following short video shows the seamless integration for componenten staging of a SAP PM maintenance order with SAP EWM. 

Video "SAP PM-EWM Integration"

Customer Stock
Another EWM function that is of interest for plant maintenance, is the so-called "customer stock". This is stock is handed over from a customer to a service provider to perform maintenance. The stock is then physically located at the service provider and can be processed there in an EWM-managed warehouse, but the stock is of course non-valuated because it is still owned by the customer.

A typical example could be an aircraft or turbine that is temporarily handed over to a service provider for maintenance. Technically, this has been possible with EWM since Release S/4HANA 2022 . Technically, stock usage B is used for this and the customer is the owner of the stock.

 Customer Stock with SAP EWM

Customer Stock in the SAP EWM Warehouse Monitor

You can find more information around the topic of maintenance logistics with SAP EWM here: 

Plant Maintenance Supply with SAP EWM (product documentation)
Serial Number Management with SAP EWM (product documentation)

entry.title: Re: Broadcasting SAP BW Analysis for Office report
entry.content:
Hi there,

Yes, you can broadcast SAP BW Analysis for Office reports. Here's a quick guide:

 

  1. Create Your Report: Develop it in SAP Analysis for Office with the required data and visualizations.
  2. Set Up Broadcasting: Use SAP BW to schedule and broadcast the report.
  3. Define Recipients: Choose recipients and delivery methods (email, SAP Business Workplace, etc.).
  4. Schedule and Activate: Set intervals (daily, weekly) and activate the broadcast.

 

For specifics, consult SAP documentation or your SAP administrator.

entry.title: SAPUI5 ui.table with SmartField on JSONModel
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have an interesting requirement. I want to create a table where my users can add new records. For this I use a sap.ui.table with input fields. This table has a binding with a JSONModel to store the new records. My oData Service has Annotations to generate value helps so I would like to add SmartFields in my table so I can re-use these value helps. So there are 2 options I think:

1. Can I use regular input fields in my sap.ui.table with generated value helps from my annotations? 

2. Can I put SmartFields in my table that uses a JSONModel to "write" my values but uses oData to retrieve valuehelp annotations? 

Thanks!

 

 

 

Best regards,

Axel Dion

entry.title: Re: EWM-WCU IDOC /SCWM/WMTOID01 seems not providing synta...
entry.content:
possible solution

  1. use /SCWM/WMTORD standard, allowing the external WMS to create a open product warehouse task
  2. use 711/712 like WPT
  3. Source + destination storage bin is the actual storage bin for the stock and can be equal
  4. exclude 711/712 like WPT in external WMS interface to avoid outgoing /SCWM/WMTORD for created WT
  5. use BAdI at end of WT creation CR_POST to start confirmation of incomming difference WT
    This confirmation can use a DIFW like exception and always quantity zero

By this, external WMS can sent stock adjustments, which are immediately populated to /SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER

entry.title: Re: Rough goods receipt using SAP EWM
entry.content:
It depends why you need that... You may use the Transportation Unit process and Unloading function if you want more detailed truck evidence. If you dont want to, you may use just unload or pack before GR. Also if you want to just check the goods you may:

- create inbound delivery based on Delivery note

- check the goods based on delivery note

- adjust inbound delivery based on result from previous step, pack and GR

There are many additional possibilities like POSC / QM with counting etc

 

Standa
